page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, utilizing a single thread per core architecture. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates exceptional memory performance, achieving top-tier ratings in uncached large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations, alongside a top-tier Passmark memory score. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, as evidenced by a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score and strong performance in software compilation. Database operations and key-value store workloads are highly optimized, with top-tier results in Passmark database operations and strong throughput in Redis SET operations. However, large-scale LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing for 70B models, represent a relative performance bottleneck.

The hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring a balance of compute, memory, and fast local storage without the overhead of external network-attached storage. The combination of dedicated ARMv9 cores, high-speed NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth makes this instance highly suitable for database hosting, static web serving, memory-intensive caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by 32 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of memory and a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store. The server delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance, as shown by its high Geekbench multi-core and Passmark memory scores. It also excels in database operations and Redis key-value transactions. While it performs average to weak on large LLM inference tasks, its high memory throughput and fast local storage make it highly efficient for database hosting, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 server with 32 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD storage for database and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 128 GB of RAM and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark performance
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and web serving
